title: When you pass nothing
checkLabels.t9: An empty order is not charged
why.untouched: script.js is exactly as it was.
hint2.untouched: The third line in the console should be 0. It is not.
why.not-linked: The script never reaches the page.
why.crashed: Your code stopped with an error.
hint2.crashed: Look at the Console panel below.
hint3.crashed: A default value is written in the function's brackets, at the parameter itself. Not on a separate line.
hint2.still-or: All three lines with it go away. They are replaced by something written above, in the brackets.
why.no-default: There is no default value in the brackets.
hint2.no-default: The parameter gets its value right where it is declared.
hint3.no-default: Name, equals, value — between the commas in the function's brackets.
hint2.zero-eaten: An order of zero cups has to cost 0. An explicitly stated zero tip has to stay zero.
hint3.zero-eaten: || looks at whether the value is truthy, not at whether it was passed. Zero is not truthy, so it gets replaced.
hint4.zero-eaten: A default value in the brackets only kicks in when nothing was passed. Zero goes through untouched.
why.wrong-empty: The empty call does not give 4.
hint2.wrong-empty: One small cup is 3. With tax and a ten percent tip that is 3.9, rounded 4.
why.wrong-order: order does not give the right result.
hint2.wrong-order: Four large is 20. With tax of 4 and a tip of 2 that makes 26.
why.null-default: null is being replaced, and it should not be.
hint2.null-default: A default only applies to a missing value. null is a value that was passed.
hint3.null-default: If you left a check with || somewhere, it catches null too. Remove it.
why.wrong-count: There are not three lines in the console.
why.lost-p: A paragraph has disappeared from the page.
blocks.0.text: An order with three parameters. Sometimes all three arrive, sometimes none. The function copes either way.
blocks.1.text: The first two lines in the console are right. The third says 4 for an order of zero cups with a zero tip.
blocks.2.text: Missing is not the same as zero
blocks.3.text: Pass no value and the parameter becomes undefined. That is absence. Zero is not absence — it is an answer, and a clear one.
blocks.4.text: || does not tell the two apart. It asks whether the value is truthy. Zero is not, an empty string is not, false is not. All three get replaced silently.
blocks.5.quote: The code works. It gives the right answer. And it is still wrong — for anyone who meant exactly zero.
blocks.7.text: You will see it written this way in thousands of files. It looks tidy and in most cases it gets through.
blocks.9.text: safe becomes 1. The customer ordered zero cups and will pay for one. Nothing crashes and nobody finds out until the complaint arrives.
blocks.11.text: A value can be given right in the function's brackets, at the parameter itself. It only kicks in when the value is missing.
blocks.12.text: Only on undefined. Pass zero and zero goes through. Pass null and null goes through — null is a value that was passed, not an absence.
blocks.14.text: Remove the three lines with ||. Give the three parameters default values in the brackets: one cup, small, a ten percent tip.
blocks.15.text: After the fix the third line in the console has to show 0.
